text: Japan's fertility rate has plunged to one point two zero, the eighth consecutive decline, according to NHK.
The government plans to allocate three trillion yen to childcare.
Yet Nikkei Asia argues financial incentives alone won't reverse the trend.
The WHO ranks Japan alongside South Korea among the world's lowest.
Experts attribute the crisis to soaring costs and a fundamental shift in attitudes toward marriage.
Financial incentives alone cannot reverse decades of cultural shift.
Japan must transform its workplace culture, not just its policy, if it hopes to reverse this trend.
